summary: Iteration 3 converges the network. Mean rubric score 4.00 -> 4.36. All five of the Judge iter-2 suggestions were applied (S17, S18 HIGH; S19, S20, S21 MEDIUM). Resulting network: 42 nodes, 87 edges, 43% source. Motif 1/3/4/5/7 all exercised; all critical hubs above 0.30 coverage; phenotype now has both activator and inhibitor arms across two effector classes (meristem TFs + florigen); TFL1 and FUL hubs complete; all rejected edges defensibly categorised. Only three LOW-priority suggestions remain (PIF4->GI deferred for REFINEMENT, hormone-class phenotype effector framework limitation, AP2->AP1 re-curation). Verdict: approved. Pipeline ready for Step 3 PERTURBATION.

pathway_completeness.justification: All six canonical flowering pathways complete with every major sub-arm represented. Photoperiod with triple-path coherent feed-forward on CO/FT. Vernalization via VIN3/PRC2. Autonomous targeting both FLC and SVP. GA with full Motif-1/4/5. Age with closed miR172<->AP2 Motif 7. Ambient temperature with DELLA/GI feedback on PIF4. Floral integrator module with complete FUL hub and TFL1 multi-output scaffold. Phenotype has both activator and inhibitor arms across multiple effector classes.

mechanistic_depth.justification: Layered cascades throughout; composite nodes (SPL9, AP2, DELLA) correctly applied for paralog families. Meristem identity module appropriately specifies floral commitment via AP1<->TFL1 bistable switch.

motif_coverage.justification: Motif 1 Perception Gate (GA-GID1-SLY1-DELLA, no bypass); Motif 3 Coherent Feed-Forward applied four times (photoperiod triple path, FT -> {AP1, FD, FUL, LFY, SOC1} parallel routes, SPL9 fan-out, TPS1 -> {T6P -> FT, FT direct}); Motif 4 Biosynthesis-Degradation Balance complete for Gibberellin; Motif 5 Multi-Output Scaffold applied for DELLA (5 outputs) and TFL1 (6 outputs); Motif 7 Mutual Inhibition applied FOUR times (AP1<->TFL1, SOC1<->SVP, SOC1<->AP2, miR172<->AP2). All motif opportunities from BUILDER §12 exercised.

cascade_balance.justification: Sources 18/42 = 42.9% (within 30-50% target). FT at 7 activators (§10 hard cap for integrator hub — defensible). FLC at 7 inhibitors (§10 hard cap — documented). No floating nodes. Phenotype 4 activators / 1 inhibitor.

composite_node_handling.justification: SPL9 (SPL9+SPL15), AP2 (AP2/TOE1/TOE2/SMZ/SNZ), DELLA (GAI/RGA/RGL1/2/3) composites all correctly applied per §9.3 criteria. CDF1 composite (CDF1/2/3/5) documented in description.

hub_completeness.justification: All central hubs substantially specified: FT (7/3 activators/inhibitors, 0.38 coverage), SOC1 (3/5, 0.54), SVP (0/4, 0.50), DELLA (0/2 inputs but 5 outputs, 0.47), GI (5 outputs, 0.45), TFL1 (6 outputs, 0.78), FUL (3/1, 0.71), AP1 (6/2, 0.77), LFY (4/2, 0.57). FLC constrained by hard cap but at ceiling of what §10 permits. CO limited by intentional clock-layer abstraction.

topology_hazards.justification: Network has multiple positive self-feedback loops but all coincide with Motif 7 bistable switches (AP1<->TFL1, SOC1<->SVP, SOC1<->AP2, miR172<->AP2) — explicitly permitted by BUILDER §12 with damping=0.7. TFL1 multi-output adds several new 3-hop positive loops (e.g., TFL1 -> FUL -> AP1 -| TFL1) but all reinforce the same bistable AP1-vs-TFL1 decision; no runaway amplification expected.

evidence_quality.justification: All 87 edges cite curated DOIs with mechanism strings pulled from literature. One MEDIUM-confidence edge (E017 AP2->AP1) — flagged LOW priority for future re-curation.

phenotype_audit.justification: Four activators across two effector classes (meristem MADS-box TFs: AP1/LFY/SOC1; mobile florigen: FT) plus TFL1 repressor arm. Right count (within §10 3-5). Both arms present. Hormone-class effector (Gibberellin direct) intentionally omitted for Perception-Gate purity — documented as framework limitation. +1 vs iter 2.

rejected_edges_review.justification: 27 rejected in-network edges, all defensibly categorised: (a) 12 direct-phenotype edges skipped per cascade philosophy (AGL24/AP2/DELLA/FD/FLC/FUL/Gibberellin/SPL9/SVP/miR156/miR172/Vernalization -> Flowering_Time); (b) 3 Perception-Gate bypass edges (Gibberellin -> AGL24/DELLA/LFY); (c) 2 positive-feedback Trap 1 candidates (AP1 -> LFY, AGL24 -> SOC1); (d) 5 MEDIUM-confidence peripheral/autoregulation (FCA->FCA, SOC1->SOC1, FLD->FCA, LFY->FD, LFY->TFL1, SPL9->miR156); (e) VIN3->PRC2 (PRC2 abstracted as source); (f) Sucrose->FT direct (superseded by T6P); (g) Vernalization->FLC and VRN1 (FLC inhibitor cap); (h) PIF4->GI (deferred LOW). Every rejection traced to a specific rule or design decision. +1 vs iter 2.

key_player_density.justification: All critical flowering-biology hubs above the 0.30 threshold: FT 0.38, FLC 0.26 (cap-blocked), CO 0.22 (clock-abstracted), SVP 0.50, SOC1 0.54, DELLA 0.47, GI 0.45, TFL1 0.78, AP1 0.77, LFY 0.57, FUL 0.71, AP2 0.71. Remaining sub-0.30 nodes (Cold 0.17, Flowering_Time 0.17, VIN3 0.25, miR156 0.25, FLC 0.26, PIF4 0.29) are all defensibly limited by cascade design, hard caps, or peripheral curation.

Coverage 0.22. Clock/photoreceptor-layer regulators (BBX19, CDF2/3/5, COP1, CRY1/2, PHYA/B, SPA1, ZTL) are defensibly abstracted — these belong to the circadian sub-model that is simplified per BUILDER §9.4.

At §10 hard cap (7 inhibitors). Coverage 0.26. Rejected edges (Vernalization direct E308, VRN1 E304, chromatin/MAF regulators) documented in build_logs 2 and 3 with biological justification.

Four activators (AP1, LFY, SOC1, FT) across two effector classes (meristem MADS-box TFs + mobile florigen) + one inhibitor (TFL1). Satisfies §9.9 count; hormone-class effector still absent because Gibberellin -> Flowering_Time (E153) would violate Perception-Gate purity (hormone must have single outgoing to GID1). This is a documented design trade-off, not an omission.

per_pathway_audit.comment: Long_Day, Blue_Light, and GI drive CO/FT with full coherent feed-forward. Warm_Temperature and T6P add additional direct FT paths. Clock simplified (GI source).
Cold/Vernalization->VIN3->FLC cascade preserved; Vernalization->AGL24 added. FLC hard cap limits further additions — defensible.
FCA/FVE target both FLC and SVP. FPA/FLD/LD target FLC. Autonomous-internal feedback (FCA->FCA, FLD->FCA) defensibly simplified.

Motif 1 Perception Gate, Motif 4 Biosynthesis-Degradation, Motif 5 Multi-Output Scaffold all complete. Gibberellin has single outgoing edge to GID1; GA2OX provides degradation; DELLA fans out to 5 targets.
Full cascade with Motif 7 mutual inhibition miR172<->AP2 now closed. Sugar-sensing arm (T6P, TPS1) explicit with both T6P-mediated and direct TPS1->FT paths.
Warm_Temperature -> {PIF4, SVP, FT}; PIF4 negative feedbacks from DELLA and GI.

FUL hub complete with 3 activators (FT, SPL9, SOC1) and TFL1 inhibitor. TFL1 multi-output scaffold fully specified (6 outputs including phenotype). AP1<->TFL1, SOC1<->SVP, SOC1<->AP2, miR172<->AP2 Motif 7 quadruplet in place.

Four activators across two effector classes (AP1/LFY/SOC1 meristem TFs + FT mobile florigen) + TFL1 inhibitor. Hormone-class direct effector (Gibberellin) intentionally omitted for Perception-Gate purity. §9.9 satisfied at score 4 (right count, two effector classes, both arms).

suggestions.description: Consider PIF4 -> GI (+1) to close single-negative feedback with existing GI -| PIF4.
suggestions.biological_justification: E201 HIGH — PIF4 directly activates GI transcription at warm temperatures. Combined with GI -| PIF4 (E148), creates a stabilising single-negative feedback loop (Motif 2 variant). LOW priority because promoting GI from source to non-source would cascade-shift photoperiod dynamics; revisit in Step 5 REFINEMENT only if validation failures implicate GI/PIF4 perturbations.
suggestions.implementation: Add PIF4 -> GI (+1, E201). GI becomes non-source with single activator PIF4. Update is_source flags and verify damping handles the loop.

suggestions.description: Phenotype lacks a hormone-class direct effector under Perception-Gate purity.
suggestions.biological_justification: Gibberellin -> Flowering_Time (E153 HIGH) is canonical but violates Motif 1 Perception-Gate purity (hormones must have a single outgoing to their receptor). The GA effect on flowering currently flows through GID1/SLY1 -> DELLA -> {SPL9, LFY, SOC1, CO, PIF4} which transmits the signal but masks the hormone identity at the phenotype input. This is a modelling-framework limitation; Step 1 LITERATURE REVIEW does not need additional papers — the biology is well-curated. Documenting here so REFINEMENT is aware the phenotype "hormone class" slot is intentionally empty.
suggestions.implementation: No edge addition recommended. Accept as framework limitation; note in paper Methods.

suggestions.description: Consider elevating confidence flag on AP2 -> AP1 (E017 MEDIUM) via full-text re-curation.
suggestions.biological_justification: AP2 -> AP1 (E017) is the only MEDIUM-confidence edge in the meristem-identity module. Literature search in Step 5 or later could potentially upgrade this to HIGH with additional ChIP-seq/mutant evidence. Not blocking — AP2 -| SOC1 -> AP1 provides the main repression path.
suggestions.implementation: Flag for LITERATURE REVIEW re-curation in a future pipeline pass. No edge modification needed now.

literature_gaps: Hormone-class direct effector on the phenotype (Gibberellin -> Flowering_Time, E153 HIGH) is intentionally omitted under Perception-Gate purity. The GA effect flows through DELLA -> {SPL9, LFY, SOC1, CO, PIF4}. This is a modelling trade-off, not a curation gap.
